title: Unidentified B1 battle droid (Endor)
content:
{{Top|fga|leg}} {{Otheruses|Unidentified B1 battle droid}} {{Conjecture|legends}} {{Droid |type=CIS |image=[[File:MouthyB1.jpg]] |name=Unidentified B1 battle droid (Endor) |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], [[Endor/Legends|Endor]]{{CWACite|5|What Goes Up... (comic story)}} |creator= |manufacturer= |line= |model=[[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-series battle droid]] |class=[[Battle droid/Legends|Battle droid]] |cost= |length= |width= |height=1.91 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] |mass= |gender= |pronouns=new |sensor=[[Color/Legends|Black]] |plating=Tan |armament= |equipment=[[E-5 blaster rifle/Legends|E-5 blaster rifle]] |affiliation=*[[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] *[[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] *[[Aayla Secura]] {{C|forced alliance}} }} {{Quote|Great—a battle droid with a sense of humor.|Aayla Secura|}} An unusually sarcastic '''B1 battle droid''' was part of a [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] force based on the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]. Part of the [[Separatist Droid Army/Legends|Separatist Droid Army]], the [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|droid]] was dispatched along with many of its comrades to establish an outpost in the [[Endor system/Legends|Endor system]] late in the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] soon arrived on Endor to investigate, but both the Republic and Confederate forces were attacked by a [[Beast in the Mouth of the Mountain|native beast]], with the sarcastic [[Battle droid/Legends|droid]] being the only survivor. It fled into the [[Tree/Legends|forest]], but before long another group of Republic [[Troop/Legends|troops]] had arrived. The B1 tried to ambush the group's leader, [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Aayla Secura/Legends|Aayla Secura]], but was totally [[Dismemberment/Legends|dismembered]] by the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi's]] [[Aayla Secura's lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] before both of them were captured by native [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]]. The Ewoks tasked Secura with defeating the beast, and she took the B1 with her to its lair, strapping the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] to her back. Secura eventually reached the creature's den, and she and the droid were set upon by the creature. The B1 was almost destroyed by the creature, but Secura was able to save it; however, moments later, she was not able to repeat the feat, and the B1 was crushed. Secura eventually defeated the beast, while the droid's cranium was recovered by the Ewoks, and its part in the beast's defeat was recorded in Ewok art. ==Biography== ===Attack on Endor=== {{Quote|What happened here, droid!?''"
"''Uh…I can explain!|Aayla Secura and the B1|}} [[File:DroidAayla-CW5.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The B1 confers with Aayla Secura]] A [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-series battle droid]] served the [[Separatist Droid Army/Legends|Separatist Droid Army]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. Unlike most B1s, whose thinking was done via a [[Central Control Computer/Legends|Central Control Computer]], it had a distinctly sarcastic, mouthy personality. The [[Droid/Legends|droid]] had a particular dislike for [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], who fought on the other side of the Clone Wars, with the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Late in the Clone Wars, the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] began to establish small outposts throughout various remote [[Star system/Legends|star systems]]. The sarcastic B1 and a [[Garrison/Legends|garrison]] of other [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] were dispatched to the [[Endor system/Legends|Endor system]], and established an outpost on the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]], home to a number of [[Species/Legends|species]].{{CSWECite|I|235|Endor (Forest Moon)}} A Republic scout patrol, consisting of [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]], soon arrived on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] to investigate. During a [[Lightfight|firefight]] between the [[Cloning/Legends|clones]] and the droids, a giant beast, known to natives as the [[Beast in the Mouth of the Mountain]], arrived. [[Blaster/Legends|Blasters]] were of no use against the beast, and it destroyed all the droids, except the sarcastic B1. It also dragged the clones to its lair, leaving their empty shells of armor behind at the battle site. The surviving droid fled into the woods, and waited. ===Amputations, and capture=== {{Quote|I thought you said they wanted to eat me.''"
"''Wishful thinking.|Aayla Secura and the B1|}} Before long, another group of clones had arrived, that time led by a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]], [[Aayla Secura/Legends|Aayla Secura]]. Upon finding the droid remains and [[Phase I clone trooper armor/Legends|clone armor]], the new arrivals spread out through the jungle, searching for survivors. In hiding, the B1 spotted Secura, and covertly pursued her for a time, hoping to ambush the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] Jedi. As the droid prepared to pull the trigger on its [[E-5 blaster rifle/Legends|E-5 blaster rifle]], however, she noticed it through [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], and drew her [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. In the blink of an [[Eye/Legends|eye]], Secura pounced, [[Dismemberment/Legends|severing]] all four of the droid's limbs. She then pointed her lightsaber toward it, demanding to know what had happened to the Republic troops. Before the droid could properly reply, Secura was knocked unconscious by the [[Dart/Legends|dart]] of an [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] warrior, one of Endor's natives. Secura and the dismembered droid were taken by a group of Ewoks to their village, high in Endor's trees. Secura eventually came to, and the Ewoks began to speak [[Ewokese/Legends|their language]] to her. The B1 was moderately proficient in Ewokese; the Ewoks believed that Secura was a forest spirit, and that, because they captured her, she must help them defeat the Beast in the Mouth of the Mountain. The droid, however, was in no mood to assist Secura, and refused to properly translate, telling her instead that the Ewoks wished to eat her. Eventually, after greatly irritating Secura, the droid divulged its information. To the B1's horror, however, Secura decided to take it with her on her quest, strapping its limbless body to her back, because she suspected it knew more about what had happened to her clone troopers. ===Demise=== {{Quote|Do you have ''any'' Jedi skills whatsoever?|The B1, to Aayla Secura|}} [[File:IHateJedi-CW5.jpg|thumb|right|240px|The B1 with Secura and the Ewoks]] The Ewoks led Secura to the mountain where the beast dwelled, and, once again to the droid's immense frustration, she opted to carry it with her up the mountain toward the cave where the creature lived. Eventually, they reached the summit, the droid mocking Secura for the time it had taken her, and questioning her abilities with the Force. The two entered the cave, which was pitch black, but Secura stumbled and fell down a shaft, leading to the beast's lair, which was full of skeletons, carcasses, and clone trooper armor. The B1 was released from its position on Secura's back after the fall. It lay on its back and watched in horror as the creature returned to its home, intent on devouring the B1 and Secura. A long leg shot out toward Secura and the droid, almost crushing the B1, but at the last moment Secura was able to drag it out of the leg's path and to safety. The droid was mid-sentence into thanking the Jedi when another of the beast's feet clamped down, that one firmly connecting with the droid, crushing it; the droid's head was severed, its body damaged further. Ultimately, Secura was able to defeat the beast, taking one final look at the droid before departing. The Ewoks recovered the B1's head, tying it to a stick and leaving it in their village; Secura's exploits, with the droid on her back, were also recorded in Ewok art. ==Characteristics== {{Quote|I always hated Jedi…|The B1|}} Manufactured by [[Baktoid Combat Automata/Legends|Baktoid Combat Automata]], this B1 droid had [[Color/Legends|tan]] plating and black [[Photoreceptor/Legends|photoreceptors]]. Like all B1 battle droids, this droid stood at a height of 1.91 [[Meter/Legends|meters]].{{DB|droid|battledroid|battle droid}} The B1 that accompanied Aayla Secura while she defeated the Beast in the Mouth on the Mountain was unusually sarcastic for a droid, and looked upon much of what it encountered during its time on Endor with scorn and contempt. It hated Jedi, and frequently insulted Aayla Secura on Endor for her perceived lack of Jedi abilities, when she was slow to accomplish a task or was hit by something unexpected. It was uncooperative with Secura, not telling her the Ewoks' plans, because she had told the droid to be silent. The B1 had little regard for the lives of others; it hoped that the Ewoks would eat or harm Secura, and thought little of clone troopers, not understanding Secura's desire to do all she could to rescue them. When it and Secura fell into the beast's lair and noticed the surrounding corpses and clone trooper armor, the droid again displayed its lack of care for others. The droid also wanted strongly to survive. When it learned that Secura would be taking it with her on her journey to the beast, it was frightened, and once they reached the lair, it desperately wanted to leave as quickly as possible. Despite its hatred of the Jedi, when Secura saved the droid from being destroyed, it was very thankful. ==Behind the scenes== This character, who had never been identified by name, appeared in [[What Goes Up... (comic story)|''What Goes Up...'']], a short [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] collected in ''[[Star Wars: Clone Wars Adventures Volume 5]]'' and written and illustrated by the [[Fillbach Brothers]]. ==Appearances== *{{CWACite|5|What Goes Up... (comic story)}} {{1st}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:B1-series battle droids]] [[Category:Droids of the Confederacy of Independent Systems]] [[Category:Ewok deities]]
